title: CRASH LENS - Crash Data Migration & Processing Plan
overview: End-to-end pipeline for converting raw state crash data exports into validated, standardized, tool-ready CSV files. Triggered from the browser UI via the Upload Crash Data section inside Connect External Data Sources.

pipeline.trigger: User selects State + Jurisdiction from dropdowns, uploads raw CSV in the Upload Crash Data UI section

stages.name: CONVERT
stages.description: Auto-detect state format from CSV headers. Normalize all columns to Virginia-compatible standard format (Document Nbr, Crash Severity, x/y coords, boolean flags, etc.)
details: Colorado CDOT: Derives KABCO severity from Injury 00-04 counts
Colorado CDOT: Maps 50+ crash type values to 16 standard collision types
Colorado CDOT: Derives boolean flags (Pedestrian?, Bike?, Alcohol?, Speed?, etc.) from TU-1/TU-2 fields
Colorado CDOT: Builds route names from System Code + Rd_Number + Location 1
Colorado CDOT: Builds intersection Node IDs from Location 1 + Location 2 for intersection crashes
Virginia TREDS: Passthrough (already in standard format)

stages.name: VALIDATE
stages.description: Run data quality checks with state-specific coordinate bounds, severity validation, duplicate detection, cross-field consistency checks
checks: Severity values in {K, A, B, C, O}
Crash dates not in future, not before 2015
Cross-field: Fatal crash (K) must have K_People > 0
Cross-field: Pedestrian collision must have Pedestrian? = Yes
Duplicate detection: same date + GPS + collision type → keep first

examples: Pedestrian collision without Pedestrian?=Yes → auto-set (98% confidence)

stages.name: GEOCODE
stages.description: Fill in missing GPS coordinates using free geocoding strategies

strategies.name: Node Lookup
strategies.description: Find coordinates from other crashes at the same intersection (Node ID match)

strategies.name: Nominatim/OSM
strategies.description: Geocode from street names using OpenStreetMap Nominatim service

douglasCountyResults.afterNominatim: 39 remaining (0.9% - these have UNKNOWN LOC or no cross-street)

stages.name: SPLIT
stages.description: Split validated data into road-type filter files for the browser tool

folderStructure.description: Output folder is auto-created under data/ using the state DOT abbreviation from states/{state}/config.json → state.dotName

uiIntegration.location: Inside 'Connect External Data Sources (API)' collapsible card, as a new section before the Save/Load config bar
components: Select State dropdown (mirrors existing state selector, all 50 states + DC)
Select County/Jurisdiction dropdown (dynamically populated based on state)
CSV file upload zone (drag-and-drop or click to browse)
Pipeline progress display (4 stages with status indicators)
Results summary (rows, GPS coverage, corrections, output files)
flow: 1. User expands Connect External Data Sources card
2. User selects state from Upload Crash Data → Select State dropdown
3. Jurisdiction dropdown auto-populates with counties for that state
5. User uploads raw CSV file (drag-drop or browse)
6. Browser sends file + state key + jurisdiction key to pipeline server
7. Pipeline server runs process_crash_data.py with dynamic args
8. Progress updates stream back to browser
9. On completion: shows results, auto-loads output files into tool

endpoints.returns: JSON with pipeline results and output file paths

split_cdot_data.py.description: Road-type splitter (multi-state aware, auto-detects standardized vs raw format)

pipeline_server.py.description: Lightweight Flask server bridging browser UI to Python pipeline
